steps.title: Child protection
steps.text: What is the recommended initial approach for a child who appears unstable at the primary care level? [cite: 1]
steps.rationale: The ABCDE approach (Airway, Breathing, Circulation, Disability, Exposure) is used for rapid assessment and stabilization of critically ill patients. [cite: 1]

textChoices.text: Conduct a full physical examination before referral
Give oral medication and observe for 24 hours
Start IV fluids immediately without assessment

steps.title: Child protection
steps.text: What should a primary care provider do after taking a brief history from a child suspected of having a serious condition? [cite: 1]
steps.rationale: According to the guideline: DO NOT EXAMINE the patient at the primary level—refer immediately after a brief history if instability is suspected. [cite: 1]

textChoices.text: Start treatment and schedule follow-up in one week
Refer the patient immediately without examination
Wait for laboratory results before making any referral
